44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73

103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.

107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.

182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.

184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.

187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
10	
11	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
12	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
13	base change on the target.
14	
15	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
16	```
17	  X' --g'--> X
18	  |f'        |f
19	  v          v
20	  S' --g---> S
21	```
22	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
23	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
24	
25	The three main declarations are:
26	
27	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
28	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
29	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
30	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
31	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
32	  associativity).
33	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
34	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
35	
36	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
37	
38	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
39	Tag 02KH.
40	-/
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits
45	
46	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
47	
48	open Scheme.Modules
49	
50	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
51	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
52	
53	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
54	
55	Given a commutative square
56	```
57	  X' --g'--> X
58	  |f'        |f
59	  v          v
60	  S' --g---> S
61	```
62	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
63	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
64	
65	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
66	```
67	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
68	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
69	```
70	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
71	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
72	commutativity of the square.
73	
74	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
75	base-change diagram. -/
76	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
77	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
78	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
79	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
80	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
81	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
82	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
83	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
84	
85	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — locality of isomorphisms for `Scheme.Modules`
86	
87	Mathlib provides the per-open criterion `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
88	(`IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U)`) and the stalkwise criterion
89	`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` for `TopCat.Sheaf`-valued morphisms,
90	but it does not package the stalk-local criterion at the level of `Scheme.Modules`
91	morphisms. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they are the locality tools needed for
92	the affine reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (where one checks the
93	base-change map after restricting to / taking stalks over affine opens). -/
94	
95	/-- **Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism `φ : M ⟶ N`
96	of sheaves of modules over a scheme `X` is an isomorphism iff its underlying morphism of
97	abelian presheaves is a stalkwise isomorphism. Project-local: Mathlib only ships the
98	per-open criterion and the `TopCat.Sheaf`-level stalk criterion separately. -/
99	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
100	    (φ : M ⟶ N) :
101	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
102	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
103	  constructor
104	  · intro h x
105	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := Functor.map_isIso _ _
106	    exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
107	  · intro h
108	    -- Package the underlying abelian presheaves as `TopCat.Sheaf`es.
109	    let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
110	    let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
111	    let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
112	    haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := h
113	    haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
114	    have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) :=
115	      (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
116	    exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
117	
118	/-- **Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** If `B` is a basis of
119	opens of `X` and `φ : M ⟶ N` restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every basic
120	open `B i`, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This reduces iso-checking from *all* opens (the
121	content of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`) to a chosen basis. Project-local:
122	Mathlib provides the stalkwise pieces (`germ_exist_of_isBasis`,
123	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`) but not the packaged criterion at the
124	`Scheme.Modules` level. -/

1	\chapter{Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf ($i=0$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_FlatBaseChange}
3	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
4	% base change, I", and Tag 02KH (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex and
5	% references/stacks-coherent.md).
6	
7	\section{Setup and motivation}
8	
9	This chapter establishes the $i=0$ (i.e.\ \(H^0\) / direct-image) case of flat
10	base change: the formation of the pushforward \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) of a
11	quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat base change on the target. It is the
12	foundational, fully self-contained slice of the cohomology-and-base-change
13	package that ultimately underlies relative representability statements; the
14	higher derived cases \(R^i f_*\) for \(i \geq 1\) are treated separately and are
15	out of scope here.
16	
17	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
18	quasi-separated, and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
19	A base change of \(f\) along an arbitrary morphism \(g : S' \to S\) is recorded
20	by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
21	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
22	\[
23	  \begin{array}{ccc}
24	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
25	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
26	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
27	  \end{array}
28	\]
29	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), and \(g' : X' \to X\) is the
30	first projection. We write \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) for the pullback
31	of \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
32	
33	\section{The canonical base-change map}
34	
35	\begin{definition}\leanok
36	[Base-change map for the pushforward]
37	  \label{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
38	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
39	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
40	  % base change, I", base-change diagram (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
41	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L877--904).
42	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
43	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf on $X$.
44	  % Suppose further that $g : S' \to S$ is any morphism of schemes. Denote
45	  % $X' = X_{S'} = S' \times_S X$ the base change of $X$ and denote
46	  % $f' : X' \to S'$ the base change of $f$.
47	  % Also write $g' : X' \to X$ the projection,
48	  % and set $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$."
49	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Cohomology and base
50	  change, I.}
51	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
52	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(g : S' \to S\) an arbitrary morphism, giving the
53	  cartesian square above with projection \(g' : X' \to X\) and
54	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). The \emph{base-change map for the
55	  pushforward} is the canonical morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
56	  \[
57	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \longrightarrow f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
58	    = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
59	  \]
60	  It is adjoint to the morphism
61	  \(f_*\mathcal{F} \to g_*\, f'_*\mathcal{F}' = f_*\,(g')_*\mathcal{F}'\) obtained
62	  by applying \(f_*\) to the unit
63	  \(\mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) of the
64	  \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction, using the commutativity
65	  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) of the square.
66	\end{definition}

68	\section{The affine case}
69	
70	\subsection{Locality of isomorphisms for \texttt{Scheme.Modules} morphisms}
71	
72	Before treating the affine computation we record three project-local supplements
73	to Mathlib. Mathlib provides the per-open criterion
74	(\(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\varphi \iff \forall U,\ \mathrm{IsIso}(\varphi_U)\)) and a
75	stalkwise isomorphism criterion for \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-valued morphisms, but
76	it does not package the stalk-local or basis-local criterion at the level of
77	morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they
78	are exactly the locality tools used in the first reduction of the affine
79	base-change lemma below, where one checks the base-change map after restricting to
80	the affine opens of the base. As bespoke infrastructure they carry no external
81	citation and stand on their proof sketches.
82	
83	\begin{lemma}\leanok
84	[Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
85	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
88	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if the
89	  underlying morphism of abelian-group presheaves induces an isomorphism on the
90	  stalk at every point \(x \in X\).
91	\end{lemma}
92	
93	\begin{proof}
94	
95	
96	\leanok
97	  If \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism, then so is its image under the forgetful
98	  functor to abelian-group presheaves, and the stalk functor at each \(x\),
99	  being a functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. Conversely, assume the
100	  stalk map is an isomorphism at every \(x\). Package the underlying abelian-group
101	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\) as sheaves of abelian groups, and \(\varphi\) as a
102	  morphism of these sheaves; the stalkwise-isomorphism hypothesis lets us apply the
103	  \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-level stalk-local isomorphism criterion to conclude that
104	  this morphism of sheaves of abelian groups is an isomorphism. Pushing it through
105	  the forgetful functor to presheaves yields an isomorphism of the underlying
106	  abelian-group presheaf morphisms, and since the forgetful functor from
107	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to abelian-group presheaves reflects isomorphisms,
108	  \(\varphi\) itself is an isomorphism.
109	\end{proof}
110	
111	\begin{lemma}\leanok
112	[Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
113	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
114	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis}
115	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
116	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(\{B_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of opens whose
117	  range is a basis of the topology of \(X\), and let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be a
118	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. If \(\varphi\) restricts to an
119	  isomorphism on the sections over every basic open \(B_i\), then \(\varphi\) is an
120	  isomorphism.
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\begin{proof}
124	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
125	  \leanok
126	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk} it suffices to prove that the induced
127	  stalk map is bijective at each point \(x \in X\). For injectivity, a germ that
128	  vanishes at \(x\) already vanishes on some open neighbourhood, which may be
129	  shrunk to a basic open \(B_i\); on \(B_i\) the section map \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is
130	  injective, so the original section is zero, giving injectivity of the stalk map
131	  from injectivity over the basis. For surjectivity, an arbitrary germ at \(x\) is
132	  represented by a section of \(N\) over some basic open \(B_i\) containing \(x\);
133	  since \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is surjective, this section lifts to a section of \(M\)
134	  over \(B_i\), whose germ at \(x\) maps to the given germ. Thus the stalk map is
135	  bijective, and \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.
136	\end{proof}
137	
138	\begin{lemma}\leanok
139	[Affine-open locality criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
140	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
141	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens}
142	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
143	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
144	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if it
145	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of \(X\).
146	\end{lemma}
147	
148	\begin{proof}
149	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
150	  \leanok
151	  The forward implication is immediate, since the section functor over a fixed open
152	  preserves isomorphisms. For the converse, the affine opens of a scheme form a
153	  basis of its topology, so the claim is the special case of
154	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} in which the basis is the family of
155	  affine opens.
156	\end{proof}

158	\subsection{The affine computation}
159	
160	The whole result rests on the following elementary computation in the affine
161	setting, where the base-change map is visibly an isomorphism because tensoring is
162	associative. Before stating it we isolate the one ingredient not yet available as
163	a ready-made result on which the affine reduction turns: the description of the
164	pushforward of a tilde-module along a morphism of affine schemes.
165	
166	We first record three bespoke \(\Gamma\)-fragment supports that compute the global
167	sections of an affine pushforward; they carry no external citation and stand on
168	their proof sketches.
169	
170	\begin{lemma}
171	
172	\leanok
173	[Naturality of the global-sections comparison square]
174	  \label{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
175	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
176	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, and write
177	  \(\mathrm{gs}_R : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \top) \cong R\) and
178	  \(\mathrm{gs}_{R'} : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R', \top) \cong R'\) for the
179	  global-sections isomorphisms of the structure sheaves. Then the ring square
180	  \[
181	    \mathrm{gs}_R \;\circ\; (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}
182	    \;=\; \varphi \;\circ\; \mathrm{gs}_{R'}
183	  \]
184	  commutes, where \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}\) is the
185	  top-open component of the structure-sheaf comparison map underlying
186	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\).
187	\end{lemma}
188	
189	\begin{proof}
190	
191	
192	\leanok
193	  This is the naturality of the unit/counit of the
194	  \(\Gamma \dashv \operatorname{Spec}\) adjunction read off at the top open: the
195	  global-sections map of \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, under the
196	  global-sections isomorphisms on each side, conjugate to \(\varphi\) itself.
197	  Transposing the naturality square of the \(\operatorname{Spec}\) unit gives
198	  exactly the asserted ring equation.
199	\end{proof}
200	
201	\begin{lemma}
202	
203	\leanok
204	[Global sections of an affine pushforward]
205	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
206	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso}
207	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
208	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let
209	  \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Then there is a canonical
210	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
211	  \[
212	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\bigr)
213	    \;\cong\;
214	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma N\bigr),
215	  \]
216	  identifying the \(R\)-module of global sections of the pushforward with the
217	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) of the \(R'\)-module of global sections
218	  of \(N\).
219	\end{lemma}
220	
221	\begin{proof}
222	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
223	  \leanok
224	  Because \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(\top) = \top\), the global sections of
225	  the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\) are, on underlying abelian
226	  groups, the global sections of \(N\) with no transport along the open-set
227	  identification. The two sides therefore carry the same underlying abelian group;
228	  both unwind to nested restriction-of-scalars towers along the structure-sheaf
229	  global-sections maps. These towers are reconciled by applying the
230	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the ring
231	  equation of
232	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, which shows the
233	  resulting \(R\)-action is exactly restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
234	\end{proof}
235	
236	\begin{lemma}
237	
238	\leanok
239	[Global sections of an affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
240	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
241	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
242	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
243	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
244	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
245	  \[
246	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \widetilde{M}\bigr)
247	    \;\cong\;
248	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M .
249	  \]
250	\end{lemma}
251	
252	\begin{proof}
253	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
254	  \leanok
255	  Specialise Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} to \(N = \widetilde{M}\) and
256	  compose with the tilde-unit isomorphism
257	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{M}) \cong M\) of \(R'\)-modules (which restricts to an
258	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules after applying \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi\)).
259	\end{proof}
260	
261	\begin{lemma}
262	
263	\leanok
264	[Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open]
265	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
266	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
267	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
268	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, let \(N\) be
269	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module, and let \(U\) be an open of
270	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), with preimage
271	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(U)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\). Then
272	  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
273	  \[
274	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
275	    \;\cong\;
276	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) .
277	  \]
278	  This is the open-indexed generalization of
279	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, which is the case \(U = \top\) (so that
280	  \(V = \top\) as well). The family of these isomorphisms is natural in the open
281	  argument: the structure-sheaf restriction maps on the two sides commute with the
282	  isomorphisms; this naturality is recorded as a standalone statement in
283	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}. For \(U = D(a)\) it is exactly
284	  the linear equivalence \(e_{D(a)}\) of movement~(1) in the proof of
285	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
286	\end{lemma}
287	
288	\begin{proof}
289	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
290	  \leanok
291	  The construction copies that of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} verbatim,
292	  with the top open \(\top\) replaced throughout by the arbitrary open \(U\) (and
293	  its preimage \(V\)). The point that makes this replacement free is that the
294	  \(R\)-module structure on the sections is supplied \emph{uniformly} by
295	  restriction of scalars along the global-sections ring map at the top open,
296	  independently of the evaluation open; consequently both sides unwind to the same
297	  nested restriction-of-scalars towers, reconciled by applying the
298	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the
299	  \(\top\)-level ring equation of
300	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}. Naturality in the open
301	  follows because every constituent of the construction is either a structure-sheaf
302	  restriction map or conjugation by a fixed ring map, and all of these commute with
303	  further restriction along an inclusion of opens.
304	\end{proof}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}
307	[Naturality in the open of the affine-pushforward section comparison]
308	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}
309	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
310	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(N\) be
311	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Consider the two presheaves of
312	  \(R\)-modules on \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), each contravariant in the open
313	  argument \(U \subseteq \operatorname{Spec}(R)\):
314	  \begin{itemize}
315	    \item the \emph{source presheaf}
316	      \(\mathcal{P} : U \mapsto \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)\),
317	      whose restriction maps are the structure-sheaf restriction maps of the
318	      pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\);
319	    \item the \emph{target presheaf}
320	      \(\mathcal{Q} : U \mapsto
321	        \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U)\bigr)\),
322	      whose restriction maps are the structure-sheaf restriction maps of \(N\)
323	      transported through the preimage operation
324	      \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) and then read as \(R\)-linear maps by
325	      restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
326	  \end{itemize}
327	  Then the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of \(R\)-linear isomorphisms of
328	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} (\(e_U\) being the isomorphism at the open
329	  \(U\)) is a \emph{natural} isomorphism \(\mathcal{P} \cong \mathcal{Q}\). Concretely,
330	  for any inclusion of opens \(U' \subseteq U\), with
331	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U\) and
332	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U'\), the square
333	  \[
334	    \begin{array}{ccc}
335	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
336	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_U\;}
337	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) \\[4pt]
338	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}} \\[4pt]
339	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U'\bigr)
340	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{U'}\;}
341	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V')\bigr)
342	    \end{array}
343	  \]
344	  commutes, where the vertical maps are the respective structure-sheaf restriction
345	  maps. Equivalently, the isomorphisms \(e_U\) assemble into a single isomorphism of
346	  presheaves of \(R\)-modules, e.g.\ by exhibiting the components \(e_U\) as a
347	  natural transformation that is objectwise an isomorphism.
348	\end{lemma}
349	
350	\begin{proof}
351	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt, lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
352	  Each \(e_U\) is, by the construction of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}, a
353	  composite whose constituents are of exactly two kinds: structure-sheaf restriction
354	  maps (of \(N\), respectively of \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\)), and
355	  conjugation by a \emph{fixed} ring map — the one supplied by the
356	  restriction-of-scalars reconciliation and by the \(\top\)-level ring equation of
357	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, neither of which depends
358	  on the open \(U\). Both kinds commute with further restriction along an inclusion
359	  \(U' \subseteq U\): restriction maps commute by the presheaf (functoriality) axiom
360	  of the two structure sheaves applied to the inclusions \(U' \subseteq U\) and
361	  \(V' \subseteq V\) (note \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U' \subseteq
362	  (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U = V\) since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\)
363	  is monotone), and conjugation by a fixed ring map commutes with restriction because
364	  the ring map is independent of the open. The naturality square is therefore the
365	  pasting of the three component-naturality squares of the constituents of \(e_U\):
366	  the (inverse-)naturality of the restriction-of-scalars composition reconciliation,
367	  the naturality of the canonical isomorphism induced by the top-open ring equation, and the
368	  naturality of the restriction-of-scalars composition for \(\varphi\). Each of these
369	  squares commutes for the structural reason just given, so their paste — the outer
370	  square — commutes. Since each \(e_U\) is moreover an isomorphism, the family is a
371	  natural isomorphism of the two presheaves.
372	\end{proof}

374	\begin{lemma}
375	
376	\leanok
377	[The tilde restriction from the top open to a basic open is a localization]
378	  \label{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
379	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
380	  Let \(M\) be an \(R'\)-module and \(b \in R'\). Then the structure-sheaf
381	  restriction map
382	  \[
383	    \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(b)),
384	  \]
385	  read as a map of \(R'\)-modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) as the
386	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). This is
387	  the \(R'\)-side localization fact transported in movement~(3) of the proof of
388	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
389	\end{lemma}
390	
391	\begin{proof}
392	
393	\leanok
394	  By the defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), the tilde-localization map
395	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) exhibits its target as the localization of
396	  \(M\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). The global-sections map
397	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) is the localization of \(M\) at the trivial
398	  submonoid \(\mathrm{powers}(1)\) — since \(D(1) = \top\) — and is therefore
399	  bijective. The triangle identity relating these two localization maps to the
400	  structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
401	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) then exhibits the restriction itself, post-composed
402	  with the inverse of the bijection \(M \cong \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\), as the
403	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\).
404	\end{proof}

406	We record three further bespoke supports that drive the basic-open verification of
407	the affine-pushforward isomorphism below. They carry no external citation and stand
408	on their proof sketches.
409	
410	\begin{lemma}
411	
412	\leanok
413	[Restriction of scalars of a localized module]
414	  \label{lem:powers_restrictScalars}
415	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars}
416	  Let \(A\) be a commutative \(R\)-algebra, \(S \subseteq R\) a submonoid, and
417	  \(f : M \to N\) an \(A\)-linear map that exhibits \(N\) as the localization of
418	  \(M\) at the image submonoid
419	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S) \subseteq A\). Then the underlying \(R\)-linear map
420	  \(\operatorname{restr}_R f\) exhibits \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\)
421	  itself.
422	\end{lemma}
423	
424	\begin{proof}
425	
426	
427	\leanok
428	  This is the exact converse of the standard fact that a localization of \(M\) at a
429	  submonoid \(S\) of \(R\) remains a localization after enlarging the base to an
430	  \(R\)-algebra \(A\) along the image submonoid \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\). One
431	  checks the three defining conditions of a localized module directly. Each
432	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(N\) because its image
433	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) does and the two actions coincide by the scalar
434	  tower \(R \to A \to N\). Surjectivity of \(f\) up to denominators and the
435	  equality-witness condition transport along the map
436	  \(s \mapsto \operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) from \(S\) onto
437	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\), again using
438	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\cdot{} = s\cdot{}\). In the application
439	  \(A = R'\), \(S = \mathrm{powers}(a)\), one has
440	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\), so
441	  this identifies the localization of \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at \(a\)
442	  with the localization of \(M\) at \(\varphi a\); it is the ring-change core of the
443	  basic-open computation below.
444	\end{proof}
445	
446	\begin{lemma}
447	
448	\leanok
449	[Section-level counit isomorphism from a localization hypothesis]
450	  \label{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
451	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule}
452	  Let \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-module and \(a \in R\). Suppose the
453	  structure-sheaf restriction map \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\), read as
454	  an \(R\)-linear map of the global-section modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as
455	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then the
456	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
457	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N : \widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)} \to N\) restricts to an
458	  isomorphism on the sections over the basic open \(D(a)\).
459	\end{lemma}
460	
461	\begin{proof}
462	
463	
464	\leanok
465	  Over \(D(a)\) the section map \(L\) of the counit sits in the triangle identity
466	  \(L \circ j = \rho\), where
467	  \(j : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma\bigl(\widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)}, D(a)\bigr)\) is
468	  the tilde-localization map and \(\rho : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) is
469	  the structure-sheaf restriction. Both \(j\) and \(\rho\) exhibit their targets as
470	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N,\top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\) — \(j\) by the
471	  defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), and \(\rho\) by hypothesis. By the
472	  uniqueness of localized modules there is a unique \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\)
473	  with \(e \circ j = \rho\); the triangle identity then forces \(L = e\), so \(L\) is
474	  an isomorphism. (This is the per-basic-open input feeding the basis-local
475	  criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}.)
476	\end{proof}
477	
478	\begin{lemma}
479	
480	\leanok
481	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional form]
482	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional}
483	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule}
484	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
485	  lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
486	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and \(M\) an
487	  \(R'\)-module. Suppose that for every \(a \in R\) the structure-sheaf restriction
488	  of the pushforward \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) exhibits its
489	  sections over \(D(a)\) as the localization of its global sections at
490	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
491	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
492	  \[
493	    (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M \;\cong\;
494	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)} .
495	  \]
496	\end{lemma}
497	
498	\begin{proof}
499	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
500	  \leanok
501	  lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
502	  Write \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\). Under the hypothesis,
503	  Lemma~\ref{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized} applies for each \(a \in R\),
504	  so the counit \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N\) restricts to an isomorphism on the
505	  sections over every basic open \(D(a)\). Since the basic opens form a basis of
506	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), the basis-local criterion
507	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} upgrades this to: the counit
508	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N\) is an isomorphism, i.e.\ \(N\) lies in the
509	  essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)}\). Composing the inverse counit with the
510	  tilde of the global-sections comparison
511	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} yields the asserted object isomorphism.
512	\end{proof}

514	\begin{lemma}\leanok
515	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
516	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
517	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
518	% NOTE: the carrier-wall obstruction to `hloc` is resolved (restriction-of-scalars /
519	% scalar-tower instances feed lem:powers_restrictScalars; lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule
520	% supplies the R'-side localization). The single remaining obligation is the open-naturality of
521	% the section comparison e_{D(a)}, now stated as Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality};
522	% the proof sketch below derives each per-a hloc(a) from the top-level localization plus that
523	% naturality square for the inclusion D(a) ⊆ ⊤, so no section-level square is hand-proved per a.
524	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
525	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
526	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
527	  \[
528	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\,\widetilde{M}
529	    \;\cong\;
530	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)},
531	  \]
532	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
533	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
534	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
535	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) is the \(R\)-module obtained from \(M\) by
536	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). That is, pushing the quasi-coherent
537	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) forward along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to
538	  this canonical identification, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of \(M\).
539	
540	  This is the classical fact that the direct image of a quasi-coherent sheaf along
541	  a morphism of affine schemes is computed on modules by restriction of scalars; it
542	  is the affine companion of the pullback formula (the affine case of the
543	  pushforward analogue of Stacks ``$\widetilde{M}$ and pullback''). It is recorded
544	  here as bespoke project infrastructure because the affine-pushforward
545	  identification does not appear as a ready-made result in the existing
546	  quasi-coherent sheaf theory.
547	\end{lemma}
548	
549	\begin{proof}
550	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
551	  \leanok
552	  lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars,
553	  lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt,
554	  lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality,
555	  lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
556	  We build the isomorphism \emph{directly on a basis of basic opens}. This is the
557	  non-circular route: checking the comparison map on basic opens yields the object
558	  isomorphism, and quasi-coherence of the pushforward is then a corollary, not a
559	  prerequisite. (One must not reverse this order: the global-sections comparison
560	  \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{M}\bigr) \cong
561	  \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) of
562	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} alone would only upgrade to an object
563	  isomorphism once the pushforward is already known to be quasi-coherent, so
564	  quasi-coherence cannot be deduced from an object isomorphism built that way.)
565	
566	  \emph{The comparison morphism.} There is a canonical morphism of
567	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
568	  \[
569	    \alpha : \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)}
570	    \longrightarrow \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M},
571	  \]
572	  namely the tilde-adjoint of the global-sections identification of
573	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}. We show \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
574	
575	  \medskip
576	  \emph{Reduction to basic opens.} The basic opens \(D(a)\), for \(a \in R\), form a
577	  basis of the topology of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). By the basis-local criterion
578	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} it suffices to check that \(\alpha\)
579	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over each basic open \(D(a)\).
580	
581	  \medskip
582	  \emph{The computation over \(D(a)\).} On the source, the sections of
583	  \(\widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) over \(D(a)\) are the
584	  localization \(\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)[1/a]\) as an
585	  \(R[1/a]\)-module. On the target, since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) =
586	  D(\varphi a)\), the sections of \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*
587	  \widetilde{M}\) over \(D(a)\) are the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
588	  \(D(\varphi a)\), i.e.\ the localization \(M[1/\varphi a]\), regarded as an
589	  \(R[1/a]\)-module by restriction of scalars along the induced map
590	  \(R[1/a] \to R'[1/\varphi a]\). These two localizations agree: the element \(a\)
591	  acts on \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) through \(\varphi a\), so localizing
592	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at the multiplicative set generated by \(a\) is
593	  the same module as localizing \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by
594	  \(\varphi a\). Both are the universal localization of
595	  \(M\) inverting \(\varphi a\), so the comparison map \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism
596	  on the sections over \(D(a)\). As this holds for every basic open, \(\alpha\) is
597	  an isomorphism.
598	
599	  \medskip
600	  \emph{The explicit formalization route over \(D(a)\).} The agreement of the two
601	  localizations just described is the entire content, but it must be realized
602	  through the global ring \(R\), because the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
603	  sections over \(D(a)\) is built from the global-sections module of
604	  \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) rather than directly from
605	  \(R[1/a]\). The reduction therefore proceeds exactly as the conditional
606	  assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} prescribes: it
607	  suffices to supply, for each \(a \in R\), the hypothesis \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) that
608	  the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) exhibits
609	  \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at
610	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). The \(R\)-action on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) is the honest
611	  restriction-of-scalars action carried by the scalar tower \(R \to R' \to {-}\)
612	  determined by \(\varphi\); against that structure the ring-change converse
613	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applicable, so the carrier of the
614	  \(R\)-module is exactly the one to which the localization claim refers, and no
615	  recursively-defined or ad-hoc section-level scalar action intervenes.
616	  We obtain \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transporting the already-known \(R'\)-side
617	  localization (Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}), in
618	  element-free fashion, across the open-indexed section comparison of
619	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} \emph{using its naturality in the open}
620	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}). This proceeds in three
621	  movements.
622	
623	  \emph{(1) The \(D(a)\)-level linear equivalence.} This is the component at
624	  \(U = D(a)\) of the open-indexed family of
625	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the \(R\)-linear isomorphism
626	  \[
627	    e_{D(a)} : \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
628	      \;\cong\;
629	      \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr) ,
630	  \]
631	  using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
632	  definitional. No fresh \(D(a)\)-level construction is needed: this is precisely the
633	  open-indexed isomorphism already supplied by
634	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} evaluated at the basic open \(D(a)\).
635	
636	  \emph{(2) The naturality square for \(D(a) \subseteq \top\).} The compatibility
637	  that relates \(e_{D(a)}\) to the \(\top\)-level isomorphism
638	  \(e_\top\) (the global-sections comparison of
639	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) is precisely the open-naturality square of
640	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}, instantiated at the inclusion
641	  \(D(a) \subseteq \top\): the square
642	  \[
643	    \begin{array}{ccc}
644	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, \top\bigr)
645	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_\top\;}
646	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top)\bigr) \\[4pt]
647	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}}\big\downarrow & &
648	        \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}} \\[4pt]
649	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
650	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{D(a)}\;}
651	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr)
652	    \end{array}
653	  \]
654	  commutes, using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
655	  definitional. Because the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) is a natural isomorphism, this single
656	  square is available uniformly in \(a\); there is no per-\(a\) section-level identity
657	  to re-prove by hand. It intertwines the source restriction
658	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) on the pushforward with the (restriction-of-scalars
659	  reading of the) target restriction \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}\) on
660	  \(\widetilde M\).
661	
662	  \emph{(3) Discharging \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transport.} The target restriction
663	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)} : \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
664	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a)) =
665	  M[1/\varphi a]\) as the \(\mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)-localization of
666	  \(M = \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) over \(R'\); this is exactly
667	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}. Apply the ring-change converse
668	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} (with \(R\)-algebra \(A = R'\) and submonoid
669	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\), so that
670	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)) —
671	  its \(\operatorname{Algebra} R\, R'\) and scalar-tower hypotheses being supplied by
672	  the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\) — to read this same map as
673	  the \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization over \(R\). The commuting naturality square
674	  of movement (2) intertwines this target restriction with the source restriction
675	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)} : \Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) by way of the
676	  isomorphisms \(e_\top\) and \(e_{D(a)}\); transporting the localization property
677	  across that square — via the transport-along-an-equivalence principle for localized
678	  modules — yields that \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) is the
679	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) over \(R\), i.e.\
680	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\). No section-level computation specific to the individual \(a\)
681	  is performed: the only \(a\)-dependent input is the instantiation of the single
682	  natural-isomorphism square at \(D(a)\). Feeding the family
683	  \(\{\mathrm{hloc}(a)\}_{a \in R}\) to the conditional assembly
684	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} produces the unconditional
685	  object isomorphism \(\alpha\).
686	
687	  \medskip
688	  \emph{Naturality in the open drives the transport.} The comparison assembled
689	  here, \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)} \cong
690	  \widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\), is natural in \emph{both}
691	  arguments — in the module \(M\) and in the open \(U\). The naturality in the
692	  module is the usual functoriality; the naturality in the open is the content of
693	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}: the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of
694	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} commutes with the structure-sheaf
695	  restriction maps on both sides. It is precisely this open-naturality that drives
696	  the localization transport \emph{uniformly}: the per-\(a\) localization fact
697	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) follows from the \(\top\)-level localization
698	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule} together with the naturality
699	  square of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality} for the inclusion
700	  \(D(a) \hookrightarrow \top\), rather than from a section-level naturality square
701	  re-proved by hand for each individual \(a\). This is the single remaining
702	  obligation of the construction; the carrier of the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
703	  sections is the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\), against which
704	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} applies directly.
705	
706	  The \(R\)-module structure on the \(R'\)-side sections to which the ring-change
707	  converse Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applied is the honest
708	  restriction-of-scalars structure: \(R\) acts through \(\varphi\), i.e.\ via the
709	  \(R\)-algebra structure on \(R'\) determined by \(\varphi\) and the associated
710	  scalar tower \(R \to R' \to M[1/\varphi a]\). It is \emph{not} an ad-hoc,
711	  recursively-defined scalar action. Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is
712	  stated against exactly this restriction-of-scalars / scalar-tower structure, and
713	  it is under that structure that localizing \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at
714	  \(a\) coincides with localizing \(M\) at \(\varphi a\).
715	
716	  % NOTE: This lemma is the affine case of the general fact that the pushforward of
717	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf along an affine morphism is quasi-coherent. In current
718	  % Mathlib master that fact is the IsLocalizing / fromTildeGamma characterization
719	  % (AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward); it post-dates the project's
720	  % pinned Mathlib, so the project proves it in-tree by the basic-open route above.
721	  % The in-tree proof remains valid and is not made redundant by a future Mathlib
722	  % bump.
723	  \medskip
724	  \emph{Quasi-coherence as a corollary.} The object isomorphism just constructed
725	  exhibits \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M} \cong
726	  \widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) as the tilde of a module. Tilde
727	  modules are quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
728	  is closed under isomorphism, so the pushforward
729	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. This
730	  conclusion is stated \emph{after} the object isomorphism, of which it is a
731	  consequence — no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' input is
732	  needed for the affine lane.
733	\end{proof}
734	
735	\begin{remark}
736	  This single isomorphism discharges both inputs that the affine reduction below
737	  requires of the pushforward of a tilde-module.
738	  \begin{enumerate}
739	    \item \emph{The global-sections comparison.} Applying the global-sections
740	      (module-of-sections) functor to the isomorphism identifies the
741	      \(R\)-module of sections of
742	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) with
743	      \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\); this is exactly the \(\Gamma\)-fragment
744	      comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map.
745	    \item \emph{Quasi-coherence of the pushforward.} The tilde of any module is
746	      quasi-coherent, and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules is
747	      closed under isomorphism; hence
748	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent.
749	      In particular no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' theorem is
750	      needed for the affine lane.
751	  \end{enumerate}
752	  Because both inputs the affine reduction asks of the pushforward are corollaries
753	  of this single object isomorphism — the section-level module identification and
754	  the quasi-coherence of the pushforward — this lemma is the sole bespoke
755	  ingredient the affine lane requires.
756	\end{remark}

839	  \medskip
840	  \emph{Reduction to a concrete module map via full-faithfulness of tilde.} The
841	  functor \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
842	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful, with essential
843	  image precisely the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently, the counit comparison
844	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
845	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are

855	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). This reframing keeps the whole computation at

864	  scalar-tower compatibility of restriction of scalars.

866	  \medskip
